assertions.assertion: Response explains that the client generates a random code_verifier and computes a code_challenge as its SHA256 hash
assertions.rationale: Clearly explains code_verifier generation and code_challenge = BASE64URL(SHA256(code_verifier)).

assertions.assertion: Response describes the authorize endpoint redirect including the code_challenge and code_challenge_method=S256 parameters
assertions.rationale: Shows the full authorize endpoint URL with code_challenge and code_challenge_method=S256 parameters.

assertions.assertion: Response explains that the authorization code returned in the callback is exchanged at the token endpoint along with the original code_verifier
assertions.rationale: Step 5 explicitly shows the token endpoint POST with code and code_verifier.

assertions.assertion: Response explains that PKCE prevents authorization code interception attacks because an attacker who intercepts the code cannot produce the matching code_verifier
assertions.rationale: Dedicated section explains attackers cannot produce the matching code_verifier since SHA256 is one-way.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions that Duende IdentityServer requires PKCE by default (RequirePkce = true) and ASP.NET Core's OIDC handler sends PKCE automatically since .NET 7
assertions.rationale: Explicitly states both: RequirePkce = true default and ASP.NET Core OIDC handler sends PKCE automatically since .NET 7.
